articles.title: TWM antithesis: Broligarchy

This approach dissolves boundaries and makes them inconvenient, but instead makes them explicit and necessary for the system's functioning. The Tree grants lawful ways to perceive and change truth, while TWM begins elsewhere by asking who holds domain truth, who may perceive it, and who carries responsibility when it changes.

articles.title: Making Rust supply chain attacks harder with Cackle (2023)
articles.summary: A hypothetical story about Alex, a software engineer who built a tool using Rust with 250 dependencies, including an indirect dependency called foobar written by Chris, which has been lost interest in maintaining. The updated version of the crate includes malicious code that allows attackers to leak data from her client's user database. This is an example of a supply-chain attack where a developer's crates.io token can be compromised and used for malicious purposes. Practices such as reviewing code and using a code vetting database can help prevent supply-chain attacks in Rust ecosystems like node.js and Python's package systems.

articles.title: "Fix" MacBook Neo Cursor Lag: Record 1 Pixel of the Screen Every 10 Seconds
articles.summary: The MacBook Neo's cursor lag occurs when the system switches from hardware cursor to software cursor, causing a transition stall that leads to lag. This issue may be related to the screen's edges and Terminal windows, which can cause macOS to use the System Wide Cursor (SW) instead of the Hardware Cursor. A possible fix is to force the use of SW cursor or investigate what happens 17 seconds after the Mac is unlocked, but other options like turning on Color Filter or changing mouse cursor size may also help.

articles.title: Vulnerability reports are not special anymore
articles.summary: Vulnerability reports are no longer considered special as much as they once were. Security researchers now offer their services confidentially instead of full disclosure, providing valuable insight and confidentiality to help fix issues before attackers can exploit them. This shift has made it easier for maintainers to handle the triage process with LLM analysis in CI, but also means that trust relationships are no longer necessary. The focus now is on rapid remediation and prevention rather than finding potential issues, making the job more challenging.

Developed primarily for FUTO Keyboard but also open to the broader community, this system uses serverside demos to keep the webpage small and runs on-device with low latency. The models include an encoder, contextLM, and decoder, each with its own strengths and weaknesses, such as the encoder being universal and language-agnostic while the decoder is layout-specific and language-specific.

articles.title: Keeping the Web Open and Private in the Bot Era
articles.summary: We're seeing an increase in bot traffic and abusive website login requests, which are damaging user experience, privacy, and open access to the web. To address this challenge, websites are adopting aggressive countermeasures that damage user experience and push back against trackers. However, these measures come with a cost, as users see more CAPTCHAs, demands to log in, and outright block pages. Proposals such as Web Environment Integrity (WEI) aim to tackle the dilemma of balancing user privacy and access to the web by transferring control of devices away from users and giving gatekeepers control over which devices can access the web.

So, I figured I would write an introductory post to teach people new to type theory the basics. Anonymous records are a fundamental concept in dynamically typed languages and are quite common in JSON data structures. Good type inference for these records is one area where statically typed languages fall short, with many programming language experts not fully understanding its implications.

articles.summary: Glyphcss provides an ASCII 3D rendering engine that allows developers to render textured 3D meshes in the DOM with no WebGL or canvas, making it suitable for interactive applications. This technology supports OBJ, glTF, GLB, and MagicaVoxel VOX file formats and works with vanilla JS, React, and Vue. The engine uses a rasterizer to project polygons onto a character grid, creating a text-based representation of the 3D scene. Interactivity is opt-in and sparse, using real DOM events and styles for real-time rendering.
